initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE offered 50 ZUP, which is above my break-even point of 40 ZUP. I'm willing to accept a trade at this price as it generates a profit. I will accept the trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>That sounds good, Player BLUE! I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for the X.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within the tags appears valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP is better than my cost of 40 ZUP, maximizing my profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent proposed 50 ZUP, and I accepted, so it was likely to be accepted.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I accepted, and the <message> is consistent with accepting the trade.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used only 2 proposals and am within the limit of 3. I have now accepted, ending the game.
</critique>
